claim_text: The MMR vaccine is safe and does not cause autism; this has been confirmed by numerous large-scale studies.
Vaccines stimulate the immune system to produce antibodies without causing the disease itself.
Annual influenza vaccination is recommended by the CDC for everyone aged 6 months and older.
Herd immunity reduces the likelihood of infection for unimmunised individuals when a sufficient proportion of a community is immune.
COVID-19 vaccines authorised by the FDA underwent rigorous clinical trials and are continuously monitored for safety.
High blood pressure is a major risk factor for heart disease and stroke.
At least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity aerobic exercise reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease.
Smoking is a leading cause of heart disease, and quitting significantly reduces cardiovascular risk within months.
Diets high in saturated and trans fats are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and greater cardiovascular risk.
Aspirin for primary prevention of cardiovascular disease is no longer routinely recommended for adults over 60 due to bleeding risk.
Adults should consume at least five servings of fruits and vegetables daily, according to WHO dietary guidelines.
Excess sodium intake is associated with elevated blood pressure; the recommended limit is less than 2,300 mg per day for most adults.
Whole grains contain more fibre, vitamins, and minerals than refined grains and are associated with reduced chronic disease risk.
Dietary fibre from fruits, vegetables, and whole grains supports digestive health and reduces colorectal cancer risk.
Breastfeeding provides optimal infant nutrition and is associated with reduced risk of infections, allergies, and obesity.
Handwashing with soap and water for at least 20 seconds is one of the most effective ways to prevent the spread of infectious diseases.
Consistent and correct condom use reduces the risk of sexually transmitted infections including HIV.
Tuberculosis is an airborne disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis and can be effectively treated with a course of antibiotics.
Antibiotics are effective against bacterial infections but do not treat viral infections such as the common cold or influenza.
Malaria is transmitted through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes and is preventable with appropriate protective measures.
Regular colorectal cancer screening starting at age 45 can detect cancer early or prevent it by removing precancerous polyps.
Mammography screening for women aged 40 to 74 can detect breast cancer before symptoms appear.
Pap smears can detect precancerous cervical changes that may develop into cancer if left untreated.
Annual skin examinations by a dermatologist are recommended for early detection of melanoma and other skin cancers.
Low-dose CT scanning is recommended for annual lung cancer screening in adults aged 50 to 80 with a significant smoking history.
Type 2 diabetes can often be prevented or delayed through weight management, regular physical activity, and a healthy diet.
People with type 1 diabetes require daily insulin therapy because their pancreas does not produce insulin.
Maintaining blood glucose within target ranges helps prevent long-term complications of diabetes, including nerve damage and kidney disease.
The A1C test measures average blood sugar levels over the past two to three months and is used to monitor diabetes management.
People with diabetes face higher cardiovascular risk and require regular monitoring of blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
Depression is a common and treatable medical condition, not a personal weakness or character flaw.
Cognitive behavioural therapy is an evidence-based treatment effective for depression, anxiety disorders, and other mental health conditions.
Suicide is preventable, and people experiencing suicidal thoughts should seek professional help immediately.
Sleep deprivation is associated with increased risk of depression, anxiety, and impaired cognitive function.
Regular physical exercise has been shown in clinical studies to reduce symptoms of depression and anxiety.
